releases.name: v1.2.1
releases.body:
## What's Changed

**Bug Fixes**
_Add security fix for vulnerabilities_
* [Snyk] Fix for 8 vulnerabilities by @decause-gov in https://github.com/DSACMS/repo-scaffolder/pull/379

**Documentation**
_Add new CITATION.cff file and update documentation to reflect recent test and linter additions to project_
* Create CITATION.cff by @decause-gov in https://github.com/DSACMS/repo-scaffolder/pull/380
* Docs: Add information on tests and linters by @natalialuzuriaga in https://github.com/DSACMS/repo-scaffolder/pull/381


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/DSACMS/repo-scaffolder/compare/v1.2.0...v1.2.1
releases.author: natalialuzuriaga

releases.name: v1.2.0
releases.body:
## What's Changed

**Repository Templates**
_Added edits to READMEs across all tiers based on user research feedback, new .gitignore added to all templates!_
* Change README Tier2 by @DinneK in https://github.com/DSACMS/repo-scaffolder/pull/371
* Add edits from previous PR by @DinneK in https://github.com/DSACMS/repo-scaffolder/pull/372
* Repository Templates: Add .gitignore by @natalialuzuriaga in https://github.com/DSACMS/repo-scaffolder/pull/373

**Outbound Checklists**
_Added Wiz to code analysis toolkit along with link fixes_
* Outbound Checklist: Update Tier 4 checklist.md to include Wiz by @natalialuzuriaga in https://github.com/DSACMS/repo-scaffolder/pull/367
* Add Wiz as an optional cloud security tool by @sachin-panayil in https://github.com/DSACMS/repo-scaffolder/pull/369
* Outbound Checklists: Fix bulk_extractor OSPO guide link in Tiers 2-4 by @natalialuzuriaga in https://github.com/DSACMS/repo-scaffolder/pull/370

**[NEW] Tests and GitHub Action Workflows**
_Add pytest tests to ensure repository creation using cookiecutter, added new PR check for markdown docs_
* Workflows: Add workflow for markdown docs check by @natalialuzuriaga in https://github.com/DSACMS/repo-scaffolder/pull/376
* Tests: Create tests using pytest-cookies by @natalialuzuriaga in https://github.com/DSACMS/repo-scaffolder/pull/375
* Workflow: Update contributors.yml with fix and update contributor count in COMMUNITY.md by @natalialuzuriaga in https://github.com/DSACMS/repo-scaffolder/pull/378

**Housekeeping**
_code.json is fully up-to-date, repository uses automated-codejson-generator for upkeep of file_
* Update code.json by @github-actions[bot] in https://github.com/DSACMS/repo-scaffolder/pull/368
* Update code.json by @github-actions[bot] in https://github.com/DSACMS/repo-scaffolder/pull/374
* Release: Merge dev to main by @natalialuzuriaga in https://github.com/DSACMS/repo-scaffolder/pull/377

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/DSACMS/repo-scaffolder/compare/v1.1.0...v1.2.0
releases.author: natalialuzuriaga

releases.name: v1.1.0
releases.body:
## What's Changed

#### Landing Page
_Added new sections such as open source project characteristics, repository journey, repository publishing. Revised tier determiner quiz questions_
* Landing Page: Add individual tree graphics for mobile by @natalialuzuriaga in https://github.com/DSACMS/repo-scaffolder/pull/331
* Landing Page: Add how a repo becomes a project section by @natalialuzuriaga in https://github.com/DSACMS/repo-scaffolder/pull/337
* Landing Page; Added new content on repository publishing by @natalialuzuriaga in https://github.com/DSACMS/repo-scaffolder/pull/338
* Landing Page: Updated content on open source project characteristics and repository publishing by @natalialuzuriaga in https://github.com/DSACMS/repo-scaffolder/pull/339
* Update index.html by @decause-gov in https://github.com/DSACMS/repo-scaffolder/pull/340
* Tier Determiner Quiz: Edit Quiz Questions by @natalialuzuriaga in https://github.com/DSACMS/repo-scaffolder/pull/353

#### Repository Templates & Cookiecutter
_code.json cookiecutter now follows latest v2 code.json schema_
* addTeam() post gen hook: Fixing f string quotes by @spopelka-dsac in https://github.com/DSACMS/repo-scaffolder/pull/333
* Cookiecutter: Update code.json generation to reflect current schema by @natalialuzuriaga in https://github.com/DSACMS/repo-scaffolder/pull/345
* Repository Templates: Add PR template by @natalialuzuriaga in https://github.com/DSACMS/repo-scaffolder/pull/356

#### Outbound Checklists
_Offers guidance on setting up GitHub repositories_
* Update checklist.md by @decause-gov in https://github.com/DSACMS/repo-scaffolder/pull/335
* Add pointer to GitHub guides by @DinneK in https://github.com/DSACMS/repo-scaffolder/pull/362

#### Repolinter
_Rules now reflect latest maturity model updates_
* Repolinter: Remove old COMMUNITY_GUIDELINES.md rules by @natalialuzuriaga in https://github.com/DSACMS/repo-scaffolder/pull/347

#### GitHub Action Workflows
_Templates are now synced with template repositories, fixes across various workflows_
* Workflow: Add GitHub Action to sync repo-scaffolder templates with GitHub template repositories by @natalialuzuriaga in https://github.com/DSACMS/repo-scaffolder/pull/346
* Workflows: Update automated-codejson-generator GitHub Action by @natalialuzuriaga in https://github.com/DSACMS/repo-scaffolder/pull/355
* Workflows: Add gitleaks config file to sync & minor fixes to contributors workflow by @natalialuzuriaga in https://github.com/DSACMS/repo-scaffolder/pull/358
* Workflow: Update Auto-Changelog Action by @natalialuzuriaga in https://github.com/DSACMS/repo-scaffolder/pull/360
* Workflows: Add content permissions to auto-changelog.yml by @natalialuzuriaga in https://github.com/DSACMS/repo-scaffolder/pull/366

#### Documentation on Maturity Models & Templates
_Tweaks for Tier 3 & 4 descriptions for clarity_
* Docs: Add repository journey content and repository templates by @natalialuzuriaga in https://github.com/DSACMS/repo-scaffolder/pull/344
* Docs: Update GitHub Action broken link in README.md by @natalialuzuriaga in https://github.com/DSACMS/repo-scaffolder/pull/348
* Docs: Updated Tier 3 and Tier 4 descriptions by @natalialuzuriaga in https://github.com/DSACMS/repo-scaffolder/pull/361

#### Housekeeping
_code.json is fully up-to-date, repository uses automated-codejson-generator for upkeep of file_
* Update code.json by @github-actions[bot] in https://github.com/DSACMS/repo-scaffolder/pull/325
* Add Edits From NPD Repo by @IsaacMilarky in https://github.com/DSACMS/repo-scaffolder/pull/343
* Update code.json by @github-actions[bot] in https://github.com/DSACMS/repo-scaffolder/pull/359
* Update updateCodeJSON.yml by @natalialuzuriaga in https://github.com/DSACMS/repo-scaffolder/pull/364
* Workflows: Add updateCodeJSON.yml by @natalialuzuriaga in https://github.com/DSACMS/repo-scaffolder/pull/365

## New Contributors
* @spopelka-dsac made their first contribution in https://github.com/DSACMS/repo-scaffolder/pull/333

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/DSACMS/repo-scaffolder/compare/v1.0.0...v1.1.0
releases.author: natalialuzuriaga

releases.name: Initial Release
releases.body:
This release includes production-ready versions of repo-scaffolder tools and processes:
- Maturity Model Framework
- Repository Templates for all tiers: creation and updates using cookiecutter and repolinter
- Outbound Review checklists for all tiers
releases.author: natalialuzuriaga

commits.message:
Replace git-checkout with git-switch in guides

The functionality of git-checkout was split into git-switch and
git-restore in version 2.23 (2019-08) in order to be (slightly) less
confusing. The checkout command won't go away soon, but switch will
likely be easier for new users to remember.
commits.author: Eamon

repos.name: iv-cbv-payroll
repos.description: Eligibility Made Easy (Emmy) using Consent-Based Verification (CBV) is a prototype that allows benefit applicants to verify their income directly using payroll providers. It is currently being piloted for testing and validation purposes.

commits.message:
FFS-4009: Basic demo launcher (#1673)

* Split the /demo page into two versions: /launcher, which is a new, simplified version focused on creating excellent demos, and /launcher/advanced, which is the same as the pre-existing /demo page, focused on allowing thorough testing and debugging.
* Created a more adversarial test suite for the /launcher page so that, if we share with states, we catch misuse of parameters.

commits.message:
[Snyk] Upgrade esbuild from 0.27.0 to 0.28.0 (#1666)

* fix: upgrade esbuild from 0.27.0 to 0.28.0

Snyk has created this PR to upgrade esbuild from 0.27.0 to 0.28.0.

See this package in npm:
esbuild

See this project in Snyk:
https://app.snyk.io/org/emmy/project/01df973f-e05a-44d3-84e8-1da1f5fec76f?utm_source=github&utm_medium=referral&page=upgrade-pr

* fix: sync package-lock and overrides for esbuild 0.28.0

Snyk bumped esbuild in package.json without updating the lockfile, which
breaks CI (npm install --frozen-lockfile). Align overrides with the new
version.

---------

Co-authored-by: snyk-bot <snyk-bot@snyk.io>
Co-authored-by: Ben Calegari <bencalegari@navapbc.com>

issues in most cases.

commits.message:
FFS-4241: Silence 403, 408, 502 errors from LA (#1649)

This adds a new configuration option,
`silence_errors_from_response_codes`, which allows the JSON and HTTP PDF
transmitters to raise a different class of exception upon receiving
those response codes. This exception, the `ApplicationJob::SilencedError`
then is not reported to NewRelic.
commits.author: Tom Dooner

commits.message:
Ignore "erb" vulnerability scan finding (#1670)

It's not used by our application (which includes erb 6.0.2). I've also
cleared out the rest of these vulnerability ignores, in hopes that we
don't need them anymore.
commits.author: Tom Dooner

commits.author: Ben Calegari

commits.message:
FFS-4239: update caseworker transmitter retry timing (#1662)

Previously, we would attempt to send a caseworker report 5 times with polynomial backoff. Now, we attempt 6 times, with retries happening with the following delays: 1m, 5m, 30m, 1h, 4h
commits.author: Tim Miller

commits.message:
FFS-3880: delete on exit when creating an activity (#1550)

Now, when you exit the activity flow during creation only, the records we created along the way will be deleted. As such, you will no longer see a representation of the incomplete activity on the Activity Hub when you return to it. This should work whether you hit the exit button, the back button, or even navigate to the hub manually via URL.

commits.message:
Fix ArgumentError by disabling NewRelic SemanticLogger integration (#1641)

On a page that raises an exception (for example a 404 page), these were
turning into a 500 error due to a complex monkeypatching battle between
NewRelic and SemanticLogger, both vying for control over the logger.

This is due to NewRelic 10.3.0 adding first-party support for
SemanticLogger (see https://github.com/newrelic/newrelic-ruby-agent/pull/3467).

Their docs recommend enabling only one or the other. Disabling NewRelic
seems to fix the ArgumentError triggered on a 404 page.
commits.author: Tom Dooner

commits.message:
FFS-3908: Implement backend logic for summer terms (#1515)

This is the name we're calling the summer logic that carries over a
spring enrollment to the summer if these conditions are met:
* Applicant is enrolled in a spring term (defined as ending between
  April 1 and June 30)
* Spring term is half-time or greater
* Applicant is not enrolled in a summer term (defined as beginning in
  June, July, or August) or is enrolled less-than-half-time.
commits.author: Tom Dooner

commits.message:
FFS-3991: Update demo launcher to forward-date NSC terms (#1542)

We want to adjust the NSC data so its term dates are adjusted to match
the reporting window.

This commit adds an NscForwardDatingService which can transform the NSC
response to update the term dates for the real NSC users.

commits.message:
Rewrite bin/will-deploy script in Ruby (#1513)

This bash script was getting a bit too crazy; it's time to bring out a
more expressive scripting language. Anyone know one?

The inciting event was that the bash script uses the `mapfile` builtin,
which is only available in Bash 4+, which my macOS laptop does not have
(Sequoia). I tried to replace it with something else, but it made the
script even more complex with file descriptor redirection.
commits.author: Tom Dooner

commits.message:
FFS-3772: Fix crash when there's no Argyle data in Activity flow (#1500)

When Argyle returns no data within the reporting range, the zero-state
info message was erroring because the `report_data_range` helper assumed
a standard data window size (90 or 182 days for CbvFlow objects). But,
ActivityFlows have their window size determined by the reporting window.
So we should just not use that helper (also because it is weird UX to
say, e.g., "We couldn't find any income for you in the last 59 days").

This commit removes the calls to `report_data_range` in the activities
flow, thus preventing the error, and improves the error message on that
page and the activity hub page.
commits.author: Tom Dooner

commits.message:
FFS-3837: Allow SFTP transmission with SSH key authentication (#1508)

We want to implement SSH key-based authentication for a new state
partner. This commit adds the ability to specify a private key via the
YAML file to use for SFTP authentication.

The private key must start with the "-----BEGIN OPENSSH PRIVATE
KEY-----" header and have its contents joined onto one line with "\n"
characters.
commits.author: Tom Dooner

commits.message:
FFS-3875: Separate Emmy Income controllers/views from Emmy CE (#1477)

We previously were sharing controllers and views between Emmy Income
(formerly "CBV") and Emmy CE. The controllers and views had conditionals
to alter their appearance where customizations were necessary for that
type of flow.

We forecast that the quantity and scope of these customizations will
increase over time, and also wish to prevent change risk of affecting
CBV. Therefore, this commit duplicates these previously-shared
controllers and views:
* Cbv::EmployerSearchesController
* Cbv::SynchronizationsController
* Cbv::SynchronizationFailuresController
* Cbv::PaymentDetailsController

Tests are copied as well.
commits.author: Tom Dooner

commits.message:
FFS-3749: Fix three leaky Activity -> CBV flow links (#1454)

There were three places where users in the Activity flow could "leak" into
a CBV flow accidentally:

1. The /synchronization_failures page. I updated this page to have the
   same fallback logic in the Activity flow as on the employer search
   page.
2. The /employer_search "already linked" behavior. I updated the
   Api::ArgyleController to redirect the user based on the
   @flow_navigator to keep the user in the correct flow.
3. The session timeout page would sometimes use a CBV flow's
   client_agency_id, thus giving an ActivityFlow user a generic link
   into CBV.

I also did an audit for all other ways that we direct users to the
shared income pages and updated them to use the flow navigators as well.
commits.author: Tom Dooner

commits.message:
Fix progress indicator bar on Webkit browsers (#1444)

The progress indicator used `attr()` to dynamically set the width of the
`::before` pseudo-element in order to fill up the progress bar. This
turns out to be a bit too fancy for Safari and [a lot of other
browsers][1].

This commit replaces the pseudo-element with a standard `<div>` that
gets a width assigned the old-fashioned way, via inline styling.

[1]: https://caniuse.com/css3-attr
commits.author: Tom Dooner

commits.message:
Add more attributes to SyncWaitTime alerting event (#1413)

We use this event to track the sync duration. It'd be nice to be able to
group it by flow ID (or Aggregator Account ID) so we don't keep
overindex on one slow sync.

This commit adds some more metadata to this event.
commits.author: Tom Dooner

commits.message:
Fix ActiveJob retry logic (#1415)

If a `rescue_from` block catches an execption from a job, that job will
not be retried.

This commit switches the error handling into an `around_perform` block,
which, according to my experiments, does actually result in the
`retry_on` logic being applied.
commits.author: Tom Dooner

commits.message:
FFS-3758: Fix employer search button disabled after "back" (#1355)

This seems to be caused by the Turbo cache saving the frame with the
disabled button and our use of action="advance" (meaning the user can
press the Back button to get back to it).

The stimulus controller is reconnected every time, let's just re-enable
this button here. I tried to reproduce this on other buttons in the app,
to no avail, so I think it's only this place that needs the fix.
commits.author: Tom Dooner

commits.message:
FFS-3611: Never time out users on /synchronizations (#1356)

This works surprisingly well - by simply not rendering the timeout
modal, the synchronizations page can loop indefinitely. This will allow
users to remain on this page for >30 minutes, should they be so
determined.

I tested this locally by setting the timeout to 2 minutes (with a 1
minute warning period) and then disabling Ngrok during the sync (to make
it hang indefinitely). I was never timed out.
commits.author: Tom Dooner

commits.message:
FFS-3711: Add progress calculations to employment flow (#1333)

Now, the payment_details page will trigger a progress calculator check when the user navigates away. If the user has met the 80h/month threshold OR the $580/m threshold for ALL months in the reporting period, they will be routed to the summary page. Otherwise, they'll be taken back to the activity hub.

commits.message:
Update E2E test mocks to stub Argyle webhook signature (#1328)

The webhook signature was computed based on the value of
ENV["ARGYLE_SANDBOX_WEBHOOK_SECRET"], which, when I originally recorded
the E2E fixtures, was blank. (That was necessary for it to work in CI.)
However, for other engineers that may have this value set, the E2E tests
would fail webhook signature validation.

By using the "replacements" functionality of the NgrokRequestReplayer,
we can instead store this as a placeholder which gets dynamically
replaced with a webhook signature based on the local value of
ARGYLE_SANDBOX_WEBHOOK_SECRET, giving it the best chance to work
regardless of value for that configuration.
commits.author: Tom Dooner

commits.message:
FFS-3525: Add controller name to structured logging messages (#1281)

Out of the box, the semantic logging gem logs lines like this:

> "Completed #show"

This is not so easy to scan in NewRelic, since the context of which
controller was being rendered is missing.

It looks like we can customize this. This commit updates the format to
be something like:

> "Completed Cbv::EntriesController#show"
commits.author: Tom Dooner

commits.message:
Remove `awscli` from production images (#1282)

There is a security vulnerability in `awscli`. I considered ignoring the
vulnerability, but actually a better approach might be for us to just
remove the AWS CLI from the container images, since we don't actually
use it.

The only potential use would be from ad-hoc container sessions we use
for debugging, but we can just reinstall it if we need it in that case.
commits.author: Tom Dooner

commits.message:
FFS-3525: Add rails_semantic_logger for structured logging (#1256)

* FFS-3525: Add rails_semantic_logger for structured logging

This is the first in a series of experimental PRs to convert our logging
methodology to use structured events. I tried lograge but since it is
so opinionated about logging one-line-per-request, I decided to go with
rails_semantic_logger instead, which is a more general-purpose formatter
for the Rails.logger.

It outputs log lines with associated context, like this:

> timestamp="2026-01-07T22:11:09.875832Z" level="info"
> name="Cbv::GenericLinksController" message="Completed #show"
> duration="135.1ms" duration_ms=135.05499982833862 tag="success"
> request_id="ad7f83dc-a49a-4678-8a5b-db17a0197a63"
> foo="IvCbvPayroll::Application" controller="Cbv::GenericLinksController"
> action="show" params="{\"client_agency_id\" =\u003e \"sandbox\"}"
> format="HTML" method="GET" path="/cbv/links/sandbox" status=302
> view_runtime=0.0 db_runtime=54.79 queries_count=5 cached_queries_count=0
> allocations=82461 status_message="Found"

(I broke it into multiple lines for readability in this message.)

I will in later commits remove the conditional enablement of this,
experiment with parsing these logs in NewRelic, and possibly refactor
the way we add additional tags to it.
commits.author: Tom Dooner

commits.message:
FFS-3491: Add benchmark:workers rake task (#1238)

This rake task will enqueue about 200 user's worth of jobs, and then
time how long it takes the queue to work them off. That will give us an
overall "jobs/minute" throughput estimate based the representative mix
of event types.

We can then compare that throughput to the average job enqueue rate for
certain numbers of users to find the level beyond which the queue will
grow without bound.
commits.author: Tom Dooner

commits.message:
Fix agency detection to prefer `@cbv_flow` again (#1189)

I noticed a problem in local dev where the agency wasn't being set
correctly: although a "sandbox" CbvFlow was created for my flow, the
`current_agency` method was returning "az_des". This was due to a
misconfiguration of domain names in my local development environment,
but the bigger issue is that we should always be preferring the
`@cbv_flow`'s agency to the domain name.

That's the intent of the logic in the `current_agency` method, at least,
but in the flow refactor (93c23fee), we moved the `current_agency`
into `ApplicationController`. Since `ApplicationController` calls
`current_agency` in the `check_if_pilot_ended` before_action, the child
controller has not yet set `@cbv_flow`.

We should enforce the `current_agency` based on the `@cbv_flow` if
present. This commit updates the logic to do so by moving the
`check_if_pilot_ended` logic into the CBV flow base controller, so it
can run after `set_cbv_flow`.
commits.author: Tom Dooner

commits.message:
Refactor `AggregatorDataHelper` logic into service object (#1149)

The `Cbv::AggregatorDataHelper` has been a thorn in our side for a
while: it has become the standard way to instantiate an AggregatorReport
class and therefore has been mixed into seven places.

That's not optimal because the AggregatorDataHelper operates via side
effects - it requires an `agency_config` and `@cbv_flow` to be defined
wherever it's mixed in, and it does its work by setting the
`@aggregator_report` instance variable.

This commit is a straight-up relocation of its logic into a service
object, to make it easier to instantiate reports in future locations.
commits.author: Tom Dooner

commits.message:
FFS-3579: Fix /synchronization_failures to link to /other_jobs (#1134)

For users who successfully link a payroll account before arriving at
/synchronization_failures for a second account, we want to allow them to
continue. However, by taking them to /applicant_information, it skips
the /other_jobs page.

This caused an error with LA since we assumed this field would always
have a value. To ensure the user always enters this value, let's
redirect to /other_jobs instead.

While here, I've added a `margin-bottom-0` to make the button sizes
consistent, since there is a global styling that removes the
margin-bottom from the second button. And I added a `<title>` tag.
commits.author: Tom Dooner

commits.message:
FFS-3578: Coerce JSON API `pay_gross` to zero if null (#1133)

We told LA that this field was always going to be present (in the JSON
schema we sent them). A user just had a paystub with no pay, and Argyle
returned it with a `null` value for `gross_pay_amount`.

The paystub had `earnings` entries corresponding to 12 hours worked for
"Unscheduled Time Off No Pay". Therefore, coercing to zero seems like an
accurate workaround.
commits.author: Tom Dooner

commits.message:
FFS-3562: guard nil-able variables (#1129)

In summarize_by_employer, we may return nil for some variables. Now, we protect against accessing those with the safe navigation operator.

commits.message:
FFS-3427: Use different task definition "family" for solid_queue (#1111)

We've gotten errors from AWS when deploying:

> Too many concurrent attempts to create a new revision of the specified
> family

This is because both task definitions (for the app process and the
solid_queue workers) have the same "family" value.

By using a different value for `family`, we can avoid this concurrency
safeguard while also making it more obvious in the AWS UI which task
definitions should be assigned to which type of task.
commits.author: Tom Dooner

commits.message:
Add `is_bot` boolean to all events (#1110)

Our browser detection library has an ability to detect a wide range of
bots, all within the simple `bot?` boolean. Let's add this to the
Mixpanel events just in case we need it to sort out any strange LA
traffic.

commits.message:
Fix AWS region detection by using the config file (#1107)

This attempts a belt-and-suspenders approach to defining the AWS
region in both an environment variable (AWS_REGION) and the config
file (~/.aws/config, as managed by `aws configure`).

This is necessary because in 29e57fc I tried to simplify region
detection script, but unfortunately failed to realize that `aws
configure get region` won't read the value from the environment
variable.

commits.message:
Delete `bin/current-region` script (#1082)

Jake [pointed out][1] that we could just use `aws configure get region`
instead of trying to parse the output, and it stuck with me. I keep
getting errors from the `bin/current-region` script, presumably because
I have a different `awscli` version than whatever runs in CI.

This commit replaces the script with the simple call to `aws configure
get`, which works regardless of `awscli` version.

[1]: https://github.com/DSACMS/iv-cbv-payroll/pull/1013#discussion_r2408903541

commits.author: Tim Miller

commits.message:
Track employment counts in mega-event (#1041)

This adds two metrics to the mega-event to better track Argyle
multi-employment scenarios:

1. `employment_count` - A simple count of how many employment objects
   are returned. This is computable from the sum of the W2 + Gig type
   counts, but let's just have its own attribute here for simplicity and
   similarity to the other resources.
2. `paystubs_employment_id_unique_count` - A count of how many unique
   employment IDs are represented by the paystubs. This is necessary for
   ADP since there will only be 1 employment, but the paystubs will be
   either associated with that employment or associated with `nil` (so
   therefore this value would be 2 in that situation).

The second attribute is Argyle-only since Pinwheel paystubs are not
related to employments.
commits.author: Tom Dooner

commits.message:
Re-implement bin/current-region (#1006)

`aws configure` supports getting a specific value, so there's no need
to do any scripting magic to parse the string output. For me, the awk
command was grabbing the table delimiter ':' instead of the real value
which caused some other scripts to break.

This change uses `aws configure get region` to get the specific region
for the current profile, but then falls back on AWS_REGION and
AWS_DEFAULT_REGION if there is nothing set for the current profile.
commits.author: Jake Shilling

commits.message:
Log response body when Income Report API receives an error (#1000)

For LA, when a validation error occurs, they return a response body that
looks like:

  {"Exception":"API Gateway encountered an error. Error Message:  API Specification validation failed. Request headers do not match with API headers. Header AccountCode OR Accountcode is missing or invalid in the request. Request Details: Service - CBVIncomeReport_RVS, Operation - /api/v1/income-report, Invocation Time:1:21:24 PM, Date:Oct 2, 2025,  Client IP - XX.X.XX.XXX, User - XXXXXXX and Application:XXXXXXXX"}

Although we could theoretically parse the exception out, let's start by
logging it.

I also consolidated the 500 behavior with the other errors, since I
think we'll want to treat those similarly.

commits.message:
Fix flaky test in submits controller spec (#994)

This test was flaky because the account ID being unusually short
resulted in the request stub (`argyle_stub_request_account_response`)
not successfully stubbing the /account/<id> API request. This caused VCR
to raise an exception, which caused the report fetching to fail when the
shorter account_id was fetched.

This worked most of the time, because the longer account_id was usually
fetched first. But since we freeze time, sometimes,
nondeterministically, the shorter account_id was fetched first, causing
the report fetching to crash and leave the data fetched empty.

Anyway, the fix is simple: use UUIDs of the proper format so that
regardless of which account is fetched first, it returns the same data.

commits.message:
Let `bin/destroy-pr-environment` succeed if there's no env to destroy (#948)

If we fail to select a workspace (used for PR apps) due to it not

that branch.

In that case, let's just bail and consider it a success, since I hate
seeing red "x"s from Github Actions.
commits.author: Tom Dooner

commits.message:
Upgrade ruby 3.4.4 -> 3.4.5 (#947)

Upgrading to this patch version means we will be basing our Dockerfile
upon a [supported ruby image][1]. This means we will get security
upgrades automatically, and can remove our list of custom upgrades.

This necessitates upgrading a lot of system package versions. The only
one that seemed risky at all was `postgresql-client-17` (upgraded from
`postgresql-client-15`), but I assume those are backwards-compatible.

[1]: https://hub.docker.com/_/ruby
commits.author: Tom Dooner

commits.message:
FFS-3197: Reenable turbo on /employer_search by fixing style issue in CSP (#936)

We've had to special-case the /employer_search page from the rest of our
page navigations due to the Content Security Policy. Since Argyle and
Pinwheel both add `<style>` tags to the page, we need some way to ensure
those are allowed.

Previously, we just made sure the page was served without a CSP header.
This was brittle (we had to remember to disable turbo navigation on any
link that led to /employer_search so that the page would actually be
re-requested without the header) and less secure than just properly
allowing these `<style>` tags through the CSP.

The most straightforward way is to just pin the hash of the `<style>`
tag contents. This commit, primarily, does that. (This will break
whenever we update the Pinwheel/Argyle SDKs, but I've provided
instructions on how to get the new hashes at that time.) I also had to
add Argyle's domain to the script-src and connect-src.

Since that allows for re-enabling of Turbo, this commit also does that,
resulting in a much faster and smooth experience going between these
pages.
commits.author: Tom Dooner

commits.message:
FFS-2839: Remove "usa-prose" class by configuring USWDS (#902)

We don't need the usa-prose class after enabling
`$theme-global-content-styles`, which will create CSS rules for common
HTML elements according to USWDS typography. This creates some styles
that are not consistent with our Figma template, though: namely the
heading font and font sizes.

This also updates some other global styles, such as the measure and
changing our body font to be "Public Sans" instead of "Source Sans Pro"
to match Figma.
commits.author: Tom Dooner

commits.message:
Skip tracking ApplicantClickedGenericLink for Go user agent (#916)

We're still not exactly sure what this traffic is from, but we tend to
get about 80k requests from a Go-Http-Client user agent when the LA SMS
messages go out.

These events clutter up the Mixpanel account and delay other async jobs
from succeeding.

Until we figure out a more effective rate limiting strategy, let's at
least stop tracking these useless bot events.

commits.message:
FFS-3283: Backdate events in Mixpanel by using "time" property (#909)

We currently track events with a "timestamp" property, in an attempt to
capture the original event timestamp.

For Mixpanel, in order to backdate events properly, we need the event
name to be "time", as described [in the Mixpanel API docs][1].

[1]: https://developer.mixpanel.com/reference/track-event

commits.message:
No ticket: fix i18n tasks failing inappropriately (#819)

There were a few problems:

1. The Check Locale Sync GitHub Action was breaking on main, but we don't want it to run on main. This ticket disables it on main, but it will still run on PRs.
2. The i18n-tasks health GitHub Action was breaking on main because there were several unused strings in our locale files, and there were several en strings that will never be translated to es (LA wind down copy). Now, the unused strings are deleted, and the LA wind down copy is listed as an exception in i18n-tasks.yml.
3. It was easy to commit problems like in 2 above because we never call the i18n-tasks unused check in our pre-commit hooks. Now, we do.

commits.message:
FFS-3007-2: Match names between report and indexing data (#807)

This new event (IncomeSummaryMatchedAgencyNames) tracks, upon delivery,
the overlap of names from the agency's original data to the names we get
from Argyle/Pinwheel.

Currently, only AZ provides us the expected name(s) of who will be using
CBV, but this is implemented generically so that we can compute this for
a future agency as well.

This creates a Service object to do the comparison, a Job class that
orchestrates fetching the report data and everything, and some tests to
make sure it works somewhat well.
commits.author: Tom Dooner

commits.message:
FFS-3023: Create github actions to check for string token changes (#775)

Removed pre-commit hooks on push for i18n-missing and i18n-interpolations since differences like those are now expected to happen at development time. i18n-normalize remains.
Added two new github actions that run before merge if the locales folder has changes:

1. Check Locale Synchronization -- this uses the changes in your branch for en.yml and makes sure the same keys have been changed in your branch for es.yml. This helps us protect against issues where an en string is changed but an es string is unmodified.
2. Check i18n-tasks health -- this is the same thing we used to run pre-commit, but now we run it pre-merge. This will fail if there are any keys that don't match up between en and es, if there are unused keys, if interpolations are wrong, and if the locale files aren't normalized.
commits.author: Tim Miller

commits.author: Tim Miller

commits.message:
FFS-2688: Various fixes (#653)

Various fixes found during AC testing of other copy update PRs. These include:

* In LA, we should not say "Medicaid application" but instead "Medicaid benefits". This has been changed on /applicant_information, /payment_details, the PDF, and /success.
* In the configs, I had left an old URL for Louisiana's website. But in addition to correcting that, I've updated all the string tokens to load the URL from settings instead of hardcoding it. There is now one exception: the URL on the /entry page should still go to mymedicaid.la.gov.
* On /entry, you should now be able to open both of the accordions at the bottom of the screen at the same time. Before, when you opened one, the other would close.
* On the help modal under the company ID option, we had abbreviated "info" but it is now the whole word "information".
* Because of outdated updates to the /employer_search page, I've re-implemented the changes. Verify that the copy matches the design.
* All links should now open in a new tab.
commits.author: Tim Miller

bring back libvips because i suspect thats why the background worker started failing (#652)


not that its about libvips in particular, but this is doing the quick fix before taking time to address technical debt later.

why did this break things?

commits.message:
FFS-2754: Refactor to begin ability to remove maybe_later (#594)

i suspect this might also grant some performance boosts by removing thread burden on our application servers.

this is a refactor with the goal of first behaving exactly as it currently does--putting it into MaybeLater still, but in a way that ActiveJob understands it.

Then if this refactor still behaves as expected, we can move to the next phase --> putting it into asynchronous jobs.

GenericEventTracker itself wasnt very tested, but the controller specs poked at various aspects of it--but can add more unit test coverage of the job/tracker itself now that its responsibilities from the request cycle are way more obvious.

Also added better timestamp coverage for Mixpanel.

commits.message:
[FFS-2750] submit to client asynchronously via solid queue (#574)

 This removes sidekiq, which was kinda the active job setup kinda not, in favor of keeping everything in database via solid queue.
 Extracts out most of the work done by the submits controller into a contained class. Did some refactoring of the tests while I was at it,
 but there are some extra test cases to add in a follow up PR.

 There are two choices that I think need to be made as a team, with some tradeoffs in what that entails.

 Until those tradeoffs are made, decided it would be better to make the PR mergeable but reverseable via the ENV flag introduction. Can clean that up once we've confirmed we can deploy successfully to demo and prod with this configuration.

 Tradeoff 1: do we like the Procfile suggests run the puma server seperately or jointly with the job manager?

 In the default gem configuration, it suggests we can add a plugin to Puma that would allow idle servers to pick off jobs off the queue. Personally not a fan of that which is why I went for seperate servers in dev, but worth a discussion. (not a fan due to the 12 factor willies that gives to have the background processes hog front end user servers potentially.)

 Tradeoff 2: do we do as they suggest and put in a seperate server configuration for production, or have them combined?
 Interestingly, they do suggest that the database be a seperate process by default-- 'Running Solid Queue in a separate database is recommended'. Given the autoscaling nature of our DB and small size I could see an argument for and against choosing to do so--> wanting to leave that up to the team before making a call.

* Add mission control

* since we arent using rails credentials and just looking locally for now, dont need to authenticate dashboard

---------

commits.message:
Remove `gigs_synced_at` column (#570)

This column was mistakenly added in 7dfb18a5, and never populated. Since
the `db/schema.rb` file was not updated to include the column in the
original commit, we've all been getting diffs in our `db/schema.rb` ever
since.

A column created via migrations, but not existing in the schema.rb, will
result in there being a different set of columns running in places where
the schema is never recreated (demo, production) versus places that
reinitialize the schema from the schema.rb file (tests, local dev).

Let's just remove this unnecessary column so our schemas don't differ.
commits.author: Tom Dooner

commits.message:
Fix tests running at 23:59pm UTC

Here's a fun one. Because the `Date.current` is evaluated as the file
loads, if the test suite starts up at 23:59pm, any tests that run in
the minute thereafter will be using the improper validation.

By using a proc to load the date range, this can be avoided (so the
validation will use the range accurate as of whenever the validation is
running).

I'm not sure whether this was a bug in production or not. I'd think we
would have heard of it if it were...?
commits.author: Tom Dooner

commits.message:
Fix ThrottlingError when running migrations (#497)

We've been getting a ThrottlingError when running migrations a few times
(see [1], [2], [3]).

It's because the `--query` argument is evaluated client-side after
receiving the full list from the server, and therefore the
`describe-log-streams` call is paginating through all 174 pages of our
8300+ log streams just to select the single one we're looking for.
Github Actions' internet connection seems to be fast enough to hit the
[25 requests/second rate limit][4].

Let's use the `--log-stream-name-prefix` option instead to filter it
server-side. We can still use a (simpler) `--query` to count the
results client-side.

[1]: https://github.com/DSACMS/iv-cbv-payroll/actions/runs/13775537056/job/38524000928
[2]: https://github.com/DSACMS/iv-cbv-payroll/actions/runs/13730036473/job/38405238446
[3]: https://github.com/DSACMS/iv-cbv-payroll/actions/runs/13727774147/job/38398210761
[4]: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonCloudWatchLogs/latest/APIReference/API_DescribeLogStreams.html
commits.author: Tom Dooner

commits.message:
FFS-2340: Encapsulate Events Handling in a New Model (#491)

We currently use Pinwheel-specific columns on `PayrollAccount` to track
the status of the sync: income/paystubs/employment/identity
synced_at/errored_at.

To accommodate other payroll aggregators, and better record sync
statuses, let's create a WebhookEvents model to record which webhooks
we've received for a given PayrollAccount. Success or failure, then, is
not determined by the columns, but by the presence of the necessary
WebhookEvents for that payroll aggregator.
commits.author: Tom Dooner

commits.message:
Fix flaky rake tests by always loading Rake tasks once (#439)

If `Rails.application.load_tasks` is called multiple times, than any
invocation of a Rake task will actually happen multiple times. This is
problematic for non-idempotent Rake tasks (like
`users:promote_to_service_account`).

Let's prevent this issue by just loading the tasks in the
"rails_helper.rb" file so they're always available.
commits.author: Tom Dooner

commits.message:
FFS-2403: Token-based authentication (#431)

* First pass at token-based authentication

* Drop vestigial method

* Use different strategy to enable faster lookup of user by API key

* Don’t allow deleted tokens

* Update tests to use API key

* Add some User spec tests

* Refactor tests

* Set delivery method to nil

* Only permit service account users

* Updated test for service accounts

* Set up terraform environment variables

* Use more familiar API key type

* Remove superfluous create_access_token method

* Fix spelling
commits.author: Matt Gardner

releases.name: Major Schema Update: v2.0.0
releases.body:
# gov-codejson: v2.0.0 Schema Updates 

This release contains the latest version of the code.json metadata standard (v2.0.0) as well as the agency-index.json schema  (v2.0.0) needed for compliance with the SHARE IT Act

## code.json
- Added new information to be collected required by the SHARE IT Act: `feedbackMechanism`, `contractNumber`, `reuseFrequency`, `repositoryURL`, `repositoryVisibility`, `usageType` with SHARE IT Act exemptions
- Added a new field `AIUseCaseID`, required by M-25-21
- Added optional fields from the [GSA code.json metadata standard](https://github.com/GSA/code-gov-data/tree/master/schemas)

Government-Wide Schema: https://github.com/DSACMS/gov-codejson/blob/main/schemas/schema-2.0.0.json
CMS Schema: https://github.com/DSACMS/gov-codejson/blob/main/schemas/cms/schema-2.0.0.json

## agency-index.json
- Added schema and [documentation](https://github.com/DSACMS/gov-codejson/blob/main/docs/agency-index.md) for this index file, since it is part of agency deliverables for SHARE IT Act compliance

Schema: https://github.com/DSACMS/gov-codejson/blob/main/schemas/agency-index/schema-2.0.0.json

## Documentation
- procedures.md: Contains tools and processes to generate and update code.json & agency-index.json files, including tools created by other teams and agencies
releases.author: natalialuzuriaga

Bump the npm-development group with 7 updates

Bumps the npm-development group with 7 updates:

| Package | From | To |
| --- | --- | --- |
| [@eslint/compat](https://github.com/eslint/rewrite) | `1.2.5` | `1.2.6` |
| [@github/local-action](https://github.com/github/local-action) | `2.5.1` | `2.6.1` |
| [@types/node](https://github.com/DefinitelyTyped/DefinitelyTyped/tree/HEAD/types/node) | `20.17.14` | `20.17.17` |
| [@typescript-eslint/eslint-plugin](https://github.com/typescript-eslint/typescript-eslint/tree/HEAD/packages/eslint-plugin) | `8.21.0` | `8.23.0` |
| [@typescript-eslint/parser](https://github.com/typescript-eslint/typescript-eslint/tree/HEAD/packages/parser) | `8.21.0` | `8.23.0` |
| [eslint](https://github.com/eslint/eslint) | `9.18.0` | `9.19.0` |
| [rollup](https://github.com/rollup/rollup) | `4.31.0` | `4.34.6` |


Updates `@eslint/compat` from 1.2.5 to 1.2.6
- [Release notes](https://github.com/eslint/rewrite/releases)
- [Changelog](https://github.com/eslint/rewrite/blob/main/release-please-config.json)
- [Commits](https://github.com/eslint/rewrite/compare/compat-v1.2.5...compat-v1.2.6)

Updates `@github/local-action` from 2.5.1 to 2.6.1
- [Release notes](https://github.com/github/local-action/releases)
- [Commits](https://github.com/github/local-action/compare/v2.5.1...v2.6.1)

Updates `@types/node` from 20.17.14 to 20.17.17
- [Release notes](https://github.com/DefinitelyTyped/DefinitelyTyped/releases)
- [Commits](https://github.com/DefinitelyTyped/DefinitelyTyped/commits/HEAD/types/node)

Updates `@typescript-eslint/eslint-plugin` from 8.21.0 to 8.23.0
- [Release notes](https://github.com/typescript-eslint/typescript-eslint/releases)

- [Release notes](https://github.com/eslint/eslint/releases)
- [Changelog](https://github.com/eslint/eslint/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md)
- [Commits](https://github.com/eslint/eslint/compare/v9.18.0...v9.19.0)

releases.name: v1.3.0
releases.body:
## What's Changed
* v1.2.1 by @sachin-panayil in https://github.com/DSACMS/automated-codejson-generator/pull/86
* Update version and add new tags in code.json by @natalialuzuriaga in https://github.com/DSACMS/automated-codejson-generator/pull/90
* Archival Mode by @sachin-panayil in https://github.com/DSACMS/automated-codejson-generator/pull/95
* Migrating code.json validation to Zod with tests by @sachin-panayil in https://github.com/DSACMS/automated-codejson-generator/pull/100
* Inherit from gov-codejson with tests by @sachin-panayil in https://github.com/DSACMS/automated-codejson-generator/pull/102
* Fixing broken field bug by @sachin-panayil in https://github.com/DSACMS/automated-codejson-generator/pull/104
* chore: update code.json schema by @github-actions[bot] in https://github.com/DSACMS/automated-codejson-generator/pull/105
* v1.3.0 by @sachin-panayil in https://github.com/DSACMS/automated-codejson-generator/pull/120


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/DSACMS/automated-codejson-generator/compare/v1.2.1...v1.3.0
releases.author: github-actions[bot]

releases.name: v1.2.1
releases.body:
## What's Changed
* Fixed failing jobs due to out-of-date SCC version in Dockerfile by @sachin-panayil in https://github.com/DSACMS/automated-codejson-generator/pull/74

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/DSACMS/automated-codejson-generator/compare/v1.2.0...v1.2.1
releases.author: sachin-panayil

releases.name: v1.2.0
releases.body:
## What's Changed
* Updated contractNumber type and feedbackMechanism type and generation by @natalialuzuriaga in https://github.com/DSACMS/automated-codejson-generator/pull/60
* Update code.json by @github-actions[bot] in https://github.com/DSACMS/automated-codejson-generator/pull/64
* Add optional fields to model by @natalialuzuriaga in https://github.com/DSACMS/automated-codejson-generator/pull/65
* Update code.json by @github-actions[bot] in https://github.com/DSACMS/automated-codejson-generator/pull/68
* Direct Push Option by @sachin-panayil in https://github.com/DSACMS/automated-codejson-generator/pull/70
* Add newline to json end-of-file by @mjburling in https://github.com/DSACMS/automated-codejson-generator/pull/71
* Remove generated files from Git tracking by @sachin-panayil in https://github.com/DSACMS/automated-codejson-generator/pull/73
* JSON Schema Validation by @sachin-panayil in https://github.com/DSACMS/automated-codejson-generator/pull/75

## New Contributors
* @natalialuzuriaga made their first contribution in https://github.com/DSACMS/automated-codejson-generator/pull/60
* @mjburling made their first contribution in https://github.com/DSACMS/automated-codejson-generator/pull/71

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/DSACMS/automated-codejson-generator/compare/v1.1.0...v1.2.0
releases.author: sachin-panayil

releases.name: v1.1.0
releases.body:
## What's Changed
* Fixed Replacing Description Bug by @sachin-panayil in https://github.com/DSACMS/automated-codejson-generator/pull/50
* Bumping Up Dependencies  by @sachin-panayil in https://github.com/DSACMS/automated-codejson-generator/pull/51
* Matching Generation with Formsite by @sachin-panayil in https://github.com/DSACMS/automated-codejson-generator/pull/52
* Automatically detect default branch by @sachin-panayil in [direct commit to feature branch](https://github.com/DSACMS/automated-codejson-generator/pull/49/commits/5ceea5ca835da2eec7da08ae8e6c9fd26e594071)
* v1.1.0 Feature Branch by @sachin-panayil in https://github.com/DSACMS/automated-codejson-generator/pull/49

[**Full Changelog**](https://github.com/DSACMS/automated-codejson-generator/compare/v1.0.1...v1.1.0)
[**Full Roadmap**](https://github.com/DSACMS/automated-codejson-generator/issues/46)

commits.message:
Replace git-checkout with git-switch in guides

The functionality of git-checkout was split into git-switch and
git-restore in version 2.23 (2019-08) in order to be (slightly) less
confusing. The checkout command won't go away soon, but switch will
likely be easier for new users to remember.
commits.author: Eamon

commits.message:
Merge pull request #20 from DSACMS/fix-middleware

Fix middleware
commits.author: Imhotep Benjamin

commits.message:
Add listenAddr, validate port and tests

Replace hard-coded ":8000" with a listenAddr helper that validates the configured port (1-65535) and returns a formatted address. Log the configured port when loading config and log & return ErrRunFailed on invalid port. Update .env.example to use uppercase keys (PORT, ENVIRONMENT). Add unit tests for listenAddr covering valid and invalid ports.
commits.author: Imhotep Benjamin

commits.message:
Add OpenTelemetry tracing to education handler

Instrument EducationHandler with OpenTelemetry spans and improved error handling: introduce a verification tracer, start verification and decision spans, set span attributes (vendor, route, method), record errors and set span statuses on failures/success. Map resilience.ErrCircuitOpen to HTTP 503 and return appropriate Fiber status codes. Add necessary imports (errors, net/http, resilience, otel packages). Also fix test assertion in pkg/core/helpers_test.go: change require.NotNilf to require.NoErrorf for LoadEnv().
commits.author: Imhotep Benjamin

commits.author: Imhotep Benjamin

commits.message:
Add SkipAuth middleware with tests

Introduce SkipAuthMiddleware to inject a stable local identity for local development when SKIP_AUTH is enabled. Middleware supports optional override headers (x-skip-auth-sub, x-skip-auth-username, x-skip-auth-scope, x-skip-auth-groups) and includes parseGroups helper to handle comma-separated groups. Wire the middleware into api/app.go (fixing the previous inverted SKIP_AUTH logic) and add unit tests covering default identity and header overrides. Also update docs/setup.md to document SKIP_AUTH usage and expand NewConfigFromEnv doc comment to list supported environment keys.
commits.author: Imhotep Benjamin

commits.message:
Merge pull request #16 from DSACMS/infra/education

Infra/education
commits.author: Imhotep Benjamin

commits.message:
Add NSC education service, Redis & OAuth client

Introduce a new education package with an OAuth HTTP client (nscHTTPClient), a pluggable EducationService interface, and a concrete service implementation that handles Submit requests with structured slog logging. Split token/HTTP concerns to allow injecting a fake HTTP transport for tests and added unit tests for oauth, service constructor, and submit logic. Wire Redis into the app: add Redis client to api.Config, create and ping Redis in main, and pass the *redis.Client into route registration and the status router. Update routes to build per-endpoint circuit breakers backed by Redis and inject the education service and logger into the /api/edu handler. Misc: migrate logging from log to slog in affected handlers and add golang.org/x/oauth2 to go.mod.
commits.author: Imhotep Benjamin

commits.message:
Refactor education service and request model

Improved error handling and logging in the education service, updated HTTP request headers to use JSON, and added redirect handling. The education request model now marks several fields as omitempty for cleaner JSON serialization. Test handler updated to use config values and realistic test data.
commits.author: Imhotep Benjamin

commits.message:
Add 'terms' field to education request and improve logging

Introduces a new 'terms' field to the education request model and updates the test handler to use it. Enhances logging in the education service for better traceability, adjusts request headers, and refines the authorization header formatting.
commits.author: Imhotep Benjamin

commits.message:
Refactor NSC config and improve error handling

Renames NSC BaseURL to SubmitURL in config and model for clarity. Enhances error messages and logging in the education service and handler for better debugging. Adds SKIP_AUTH to .env.example and logs related config in main.go.
commits.author: Imhotep Benjamin

commits.message:
Add education verification service and API endpoint

Introduces a new education verification service with OAuth token generation, request/response models, and a test endpoint. Adds route registration for the new /api/edu endpoint, updates configuration to support NSC credentials, and refactors related config and helper code for extensibility.
commits.author: Imhotep Benjamin

commits.author: Imhotep Benjamin

commits.message: Update api/app.go
commits.author: Jake Shilling

commits.message:
Replace slog with custom logger in main.go

Removed usage of slog and switched to core.NewLogger for error logging in main.go. This centralizes logging and prepares for future logger customization.
commits.author: Imhotep Benjamin

commits.message:
Refactor Config struct to embed core.Config as Core

Replaces the embedded core.Config in api.Config with a named Core field. Updates all references to use cfg.Core and adjusts initialization in main.go accordingly for improved clarity and maintainability.
commits.author: Imhotep Benjamin

commits.message:
Add structured logging with slog to API and Redis

Refactored API, middleware, circuit breaker, and Redis client to use slog for structured logging. Logger is now passed through constructors and attached to components for improved observability. Updated tests and router wiring to accommodate logger injection. Improved circuit breaker middleware comments and error handling.
commits.author: Imhotep Benjamin

commits.message:
Refactor config and add circuit breaker middleware

Refactored the core config package by splitting models, helpers, and functional options into separate files for better maintainability. Added a circuit breaker middleware for Fiber routes and integrated it into the /status endpoint. Enhanced Redis client with OpenTelemetry tracing and metrics instrumentation. Improved logging in the circuit breaker and Redis client for error cases.
commits.author: Imhotep Benjamin

commits.message:
Merge pull request #13 from DSACMS/infra/package-choice

Infra/package choice
commits.author: Imhotep Benjamin

commits.message:
Rename FunctionTernary to FuncTernary

Simplified Function Ternary name
commits.author: Imhotep Benjamin

commits.message:
Add generic ternary and function ternary utilities

Introduces Ternary and FunctionTernary functions in pkg/choice/choice.go to provide generic ternary operator functionality for values and functions.
commits.author: Imhotep Benjamin

commits.message:
Refactor otel_test.go to use testify assertions

Replaces manual error checks in TestOtel_EmitsSpan with testify's require.Len and assert.Equal for improved readability and consistency in test assertions.
commits.author: Imhotep Benjamin

commits.message:
Refactor Redis config to use core.Config struct

Moved Redis configuration from environment variables to the core.Config struct, updating StatusRouter and related tests to accept Redis settings from config. Added RedisConfig to core.Config, with helper functions and environment variable support. Improved context handling in status handler and cleaned up middleware comments. Updated circuit breaker tests and added interface assertion for RedisBreaker.
commits.author: Imhotep Benjamin

commits.message: removing dump.rdb  from Git
commits.author: Imhotep Benjamin

commits.message: removing .DS_Store from Git
commits.author: Imhotep Benjamin

commits.message:
Refactor Redis circuit breaker state management

The Redis circuit breaker now stores the time to half-open in the state key instead of a simple open flag, improving accuracy for state transitions. Key naming and cleanup logic were updated, and tests were adjusted to match the new state key semantics. The Redis client now uses configuration values for address, password, and DB instead of hardcoded defaults.
commits.author: Imhotep Benjamin

commits.message:
Refactor Redis circuit breaker and status endpoint

Refactored the Redis circuit breaker to remove Lua scripting and simplify key management, updating method signatures and tests accordingly. The status endpoint now checks Redis connectivity using a new handler. Updated dependencies and configuration handling for improved clarity and maintainability.
commits.author: Imhotep Benjamin

commits.message:
Add Redis client package and dependencies

Introduced a new pkg/redis/redis.go file providing a Redis client constructor and ping function using go-redis v9. Updated go.mod and go.sum to include go-redis and related dependencies. Added .DS_Store and dump.rdb files.
commits.author: Imhotep Benjamin

commits.message: Merge branch 'main' into infra/circuit-breaker
commits.author: Imhotep Benjamin

commits.message:
Refactor: move circuitbreaker and redis packages

Moved circuitbreaker and redis packages from pkg/core/ to pkg/ for improved project structure. Updated .gitignore to exclude .DS_Store and dump.rdb files. Removed .DS_Store and dump.rdb from the repository.
commits.author: Imhotep Benjamin

commits.message:
Add and expand RedisBreaker unit tests

Refactored test setup into helper functions and added new tests for key generation, Allow method, and OnFailure state transitions in RedisBreaker. Improved assertion messages for clarity.
commits.author: Imhotep Benjamin

commits.message:
Add Redis-backed circuit breaker with tests

Implements a Redis-based circuit breaker with Allow, OnSuccess, and OnFailure methods. Adds unit tests for both the circuit breaker options and RedisBreaker initialization. Introduces error handling for open circuits and fail-open behavior when Redis is unavailable.
commits.author: Imhotep Benjamin

commits.message:
Merge pull request #11 from DSACMS/add-testing

Refactor env loading and improve tests
commits.author: Imhotep Benjamin

commits.message:
Refactor env loading and improve tests

Refactored LoadEnv to fix environment-specific file naming and error handling. Updated tests in config_test.go and status_router_test.go to use testify for better assertions and error reporting. Updated dependencies in go.mod and go.sum, including adding testify and upgrading several packages.
commits.author: Imhotep Benjamin

commits.message:
Add Redis-backed circuit breaker and Redis client

Introduces a Redis-backed circuit breaker implementation with configurable options and Lua scripting for atomic state management. Adds a Redis client wrapper with configuration, connection, and basic health check, along with unit tests for Redis connectivity and operations. Updates dependencies to include go-redis and testify for testing.
commits.author: Imhotep Benjamin

releases.name: v1.0.0
releases.body:
# repo-sunsetter
repo-sunsetter is a GitHub Action that prepares a repository for archival by automating documentation updates and providing an archival review checklist.

## What's Changed
* Archival Checklists: Add the 2 archival checklists with README explainer by @natalialuzuriaga in https://github.com/DSACMS/repo-sunsetter/pull/2
* Created repo-sunsetter GH Action MVP!! by @natalialuzuriaga in https://github.com/DSACMS/repo-sunsetter/pull/4
* Docs: Add repository documentation by @natalialuzuriaga in https://github.com/DSACMS/repo-sunsetter/pull/8
* Checklists: Provide template checklist and specify CMS.gov checklists by @natalialuzuriaga in https://github.com/DSACMS/repo-sunsetter/pull/9
* Update README.md by @decause-gov in https://github.com/DSACMS/repo-sunsetter/pull/11
* ACTION: Generalizing action to be more configurable by @natalialuzuriaga in https://github.com/DSACMS/repo-sunsetter/pull/10
* docs: update contributors information by @github-actions[bot] in https://github.com/DSACMS/repo-sunsetter/pull/17
* Update code.json by @github-actions[bot] in https://github.com/DSACMS/repo-sunsetter/pull/7
* Action: Add outputs by @natalialuzuriaga in https://github.com/DSACMS/repo-sunsetter/pull/18
* Tests: Add unit tests by @natalialuzuriaga in https://github.com/DSACMS/repo-sunsetter/pull/19
* Docs: Update docs and set up issue templates in preparation for release by @natalialuzuriaga in https://github.com/DSACMS/repo-sunsetter/pull/20
* Update code.json by @github-actions[bot] in https://github.com/DSACMS/repo-sunsetter/pull/22
* Docs: Add release and tests badges to README.md by @natalialuzuriaga in https://github.com/DSACMS/repo-sunsetter/pull/23

## New Contributors
* @natalialuzuriaga made their first contribution in https://github.com/DSACMS/repo-sunsetter/pull/2
* @decause-gov made their first contribution in https://github.com/DSACMS/repo-sunsetter/pull/11
* @github-actions[bot] made their first contribution in https://github.com/DSACMS/repo-sunsetter/pull/17

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/DSACMS/repo-sunsetter/commits/v1.0.0
releases.author: natalialuzuriaga

commits.message: Merge branch 'develop' into fix/6567-datalist-console-error
commits.author: Anne Petersen

commits.message:
fix: prevent console error when using datalist with text inputs

Fix two issues that cause errors when native HTML datalist elements are
used with USWDS text inputs:

1. keymap.js: Guard against non-KeyboardEvent events. When a user
   selects a datalist option, the browser fires an InputEvent (not a
   KeyboardEvent). The keymap handler called getModifierState() and
   accessed event.key on the InputEvent, causing a TypeError. Add an
   early return when the event lacks these KeyboardEvent properties.

2. validate-input.js: Move the null check for validatorCheckbox before
   its first use. Previously, classList.toggle() was called on line 30
   before the existence check on line 32, causing a TypeError when the
   validator checkbox element was not found in the DOM.

Fixes #6567

Signed-off-by: Vijay Govindarajan <vijay.govindarajan91@gmail.com>
Signed-off-by: V Govindarajan <vijay.govindarajan91@gmail.com>
commits.author: V Govindarajan

commits.message:
Bump serialize-javascript, webpack and @storybook/html

Bumps [serialize-javascript](https://github.com/yahoo/serialize-javascript) to 6.0.2 and updates ancestor dependencies [serialize-javascript](https://github.com/yahoo/serialize-javascript), [webpack](https://github.com/webpack/webpack) and [@storybook/html](https://github.com/storybookjs/storybook/tree/HEAD/code/renderers/html). These dependencies need to be updated together.


Updates `serialize-javascript` from 5.0.1 to 6.0.2
- [Release notes](https://github.com/yahoo/serialize-javascript/releases)
- [Commits](https://github.com/yahoo/serialize-javascript/compare/v5.0.1...v6.0.2)

Updates `webpack` from 4.47.0 to 5.97.1
- [Release notes](https://github.com/webpack/webpack/releases)
- [Commits](https://github.com/webpack/webpack/compare/v4.47.0...v5.97.1)

Updates `@storybook/html` from 6.5.16 to 8.5.4
- [Release notes](https://github.com/storybookjs/storybook/releases)
- [Changelog](https://github.com/storybookjs/storybook/blob/next/CHANGELOG.md)
- [Commits](https://github.com/storybookjs/storybook/commits/v8.5.4/code/renderers/html)

---
updated-dependencies:
- dependency-name: serialize-javascript
  dependency-type: indirect
- dependency-name: webpack
  dependency-type: direct:development
- dependency-name: "@storybook/html"
  dependency-type: direct:development
...

Signed-off-by: dependabot[bot] <support@github.com>
commits.author: dependabot[bot]

commits.message:
Updated forks data files:
OpenSearch Data to 2025-03-16 - six months after moving to the LF
Elasticsearch Data to 2025-02-29 - six months after adding the AGPL
Valkey to 2025-03-28 - one year after the launch of the fork
Redis to 2025-03-20 - one year after the relicensing event
The data updates used the same scripts as the original data gathering with the source being the GH GraphQL API

Added forks and stars data for all 6 projects using GitHub's BigQuery with GitHub Archive as the data source.
The queries used can be found in https://docs.google.com/document/d/1v9IiRP606cX81An105JADLVp946d9Qqmp8uhKF0PjzE/edit?tab=t.0
Those csv files are in the stars-forks subfolder.
